'''Joe Philbin''' (born 16 November 1994) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lf|sr}} and {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He has played for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]], [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.

He has spent time on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[Swinton Lions]] and the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and the [[North Wales Crusaders]] in [[2015 Championship 1|Championship 1]] and the [[Rochdale Hornets]] in [[2016 League 1|League 1]].

==Background==
Philbin was born in [[Warrington]], [[Cheshire]], England.

==Playing career==
Philbin his junior rugby at local amateur clubs Culcheth Eagles and Latchford Albion before joining Warrington Wolves, aged 16.

===Warrington Wolves===
He broke into the [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] first team in 2014. His début was against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], where he picked up a hip injury. Philbin returned for the first team a month later against the [[London Broncos]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

Philbin scored his first career try against the [[Catalans Dragons]] in February 2015, and the following week he scored again, this time against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].

Philbin played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Catalans Dragons]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history">{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=25 August 2018 |title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report |access-date=5 November 2019 |publisher=Guardian}}</ref>
Philbin played in the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2018/oct/13/super-league-grand-final-2018-wigan-warriors-v-warrington-wolves-live?page=with:block-5bc1ff43e4b05798be94cc63#liveblog-navigation|title=Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=13 October 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref>
Philbin played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|publisher=Mirror|date=24 August 2019|access-date=1 November 2019}}</ref>
Philbin played 20 games for Warrington in the 2023 Super League season as Warrington finished sixth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in the clubs elimination playoff loss against St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/23827325.highs-lows-warrington-wolves-2023-season/|title=Highs and lows from Warrington Wolves' 2023 season|website=www.warringtonguardian.co.uk|date=3 October 2023 }}</ref>
On 7 June 2025, he played in Warrington's 8-6 Challenge Cup final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Bradford===
He was sent on a month's loan to [[Super League]] side [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] in January 2014.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Swinton===
Philbin featured in two matches via dual registration for [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]], and scored one try.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

==International career==
In 2016 he was called up to the [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification – Europe#Group B|2017 Rugby League World Cup European Pool B qualifiers]].<ref name="Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers">{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/14763089.Warrington_Wolves_trio_named_in_Ireland_squad_for_World_Cup_qualifiers|title=Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers|publisher=Warrington Guardian|date=24 September 2016|access-date=26 September 2016}}</ref>

In 2018 he was selected for the [[England Knights]] on their tour of [[Papua New Guinea]]. He played against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] at the Lae Football Stadium<ref name="Papua New Guinea vs England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718006#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea vs England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=27 October 2018|access-date=30 October 2019}}</ref> and the Oil Search National Football Stadium.<ref name="Papua New Guinea v England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718007#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea v England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=3 November 2018|access-date=30 October 2019}}</ref>

He was added to the [[Great Britain Lions]] Elite Performance squad in September 2019.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49554630|title=Great Britain Lions: Lachlan Coote, Morgan Knowles and Joe Philbin in elite performance squad|date=2019-09-02|access-date=2019-10-23|language=en-GB}}</ref>

He was selected in squad for the [[2019 Great Britain Lions tour]] of the Southern Hemisphere.<ref name="Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/14/zak-hardaker-shock-inclusion-24-man-great-britain-squad-tour/|title=Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea|publisher=Telegraph|date=14 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref>
